title: Lithocarpus calathiformis
taxonomy: Lithocarpus calathiformis is a species of plant in the family Fagaceae. It is commonly known as the calathiform beech or calathiform oak. This plant is classified as a species of tree, specifically a type of evergreen oak. The classification of Lithocarpus calathiformis is further broken down into the kingdom Plantae, the clade Angiosperms, the clade Eudicots, and the clade Fagales. Lithocarpus calathiformis is also classified as a genus of Lithocarpus, a group of trees commonly known as the Lithocarpus genus.
uses: Lithocarpus calathiformis, a species of evergreen tree native to China, has been utilized in traditional medicine for its purported health benefits. The plant's extracts have been studied for their potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, Lithocarpus calathiformis is believed to aid in the treatment of various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Lithocarpus calathiformis as a medicinal agent. Potential uses of the plant include the development of natural remedies for various health conditions.

parts: Lithocarpus calathiformis, also known as the Burmese Lithocarpus, is a plant species native to Asia.
